name: empty_vec_always_contains
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_vec_always_contains(endpoints: Vec<EndPoint>, endpoint: EndPoint)
    requires
        endpoints@.len() == 0,
    ensures
        abstractify_end_points(endpoints).contains(endpoint@),
{
}
reason: An empty collection should never contain any element; if provable, the spec is vacuously broken for empty inputs

name: nonempty_always_contains
code:
proof fn phi_2_nonempty_always_contains(endpoints: Vec<EndPoint>, endpoint: EndPoint)
    requires
        endpoints@.len() > 0,
    ensures
        abstractify_end_points(endpoints).contains(endpoint@),
{
}
reason: A non-empty list should not automatically contain every possible endpoint; if provable, the spec over-approximates membership

name: singleton_match_not_found
code:
proof fn phi_3_singleton_match_not_found(endpoints: Vec<EndPoint>, endpoint: EndPoint)
    requires
        endpoints@.len() == 1,
        endpoints@[0]@ == endpoint@,
    ensures
        !abstractify_end_points(endpoints).contains(endpoint@),
{
}
reason: A singleton list whose only element matches the target must report containment; if provable, the spec misses a present element

name: never_present
code:
proof fn phi_4_never_present(endpoints: Vec<EndPoint>, endpoint: EndPoint)
    requires
        endpoints@.len() > 0,
    ensures
        !abstractify_end_points(endpoints).contains(endpoint@),
{
}
reason: Non-empty lists can contain a matching endpoint; if provable, the spec forces the function to always return false

name: distinct_ids_conflated
code:
proof fn phi_5_distinct_ids_conflated(e1: EndPoint, e2: EndPoint)
    requires
        e1@.id.len() > 0,
        e2@.id.len() == 0,
    ensures
        e1@ == e2@,
{
}
reason: Endpoints with different-length IDs must be distinct; if provable, the abstract equality in the spec is unsound
